title: anacamptis × timbali nothosubsp. barlae
intro: Anacamptis × timbali nothosubsp. barlae, also known as the Barlae's orchid, is a hybrid orchid species that is a cross between Anacamptis morio and Orchis barlae. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit medicinal properties. The health benefits of Anacamptis × timbali nothosubsp. barlae include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water, allowing for a soothing and potentially therapeutic experience.
